summary: Iter 78a LP SmartSide waste-baking regression — ALL GREEN. Verified the fix for Howard's bug where LP siding/soffit/trim/OSC lines were silently skipped by the cut-prone classifier. Frontend Node test (38 assertions incl. 9 new LP cases) passes; backend pytest regression (25 prior + 4 new LP round-trip) passes; UI shows waste-pct=20 with green 'BAKED INTO LINE QTY ON IMPORT — CHANGE % TO RECOMPUTE' hint visible; lp-soffit-type SELECT defaults to 'mix'; manual qty edit (raw_qty=null) is preserved across waste % changes (20→25 left qty=100 untouched). Live HOVER/Blueprint AI re-import deliberately NOT tested per main-agent instruction (AI budget unconfirmed).
critical_code_review_comments: wasteLogic.js isCutProneItem(): LP branch correctly disambiguates 'LP Siding Accessories' so only OSC lines get waste while .019 Coil / touch-up kits / J blocks / mini-splits stay non-cut-prone. Comment header is clear about the bug and intent.

models.py EstimateLine.raw_qty: Optional[float] is the right shape — None for manual lines, populated only when bakeWasteIntoLines stamps it on import. Round-trip verified via POST/PUT/GET on /api/estimates.
SettingsRow.updateWastePct → recomputeWasteQtys correctly leaves manual lines (raw_qty=null) untouched. Confirmed in UI: changing waste 20→25 with qty=100 manual edit did NOT recompute the line.

EXISTING LP ESTIMATES (created before fix landed) have lines with qty=raw and raw_qty=null, so they will NOT auto-recompute on waste-% change. Howard will need to re-import the blueprint via 'Read Blueprints' for the fix to retro-actively bake waste into those lines. Recommend surfacing this in the green hint or a one-time banner ('Re-import blueprint to apply 20% waste to existing LP lines').

Cosmetic: Summary card on LP estimate shows '+ WASTE (20%) $9,844.98' equal to MATERIAL $9,844.98 — visual implies double-add but the math (Base $10,534 = Mat $9,844 + Tax $689) shows waste is NOT being added as $. Consider clarifying the label (e.g., 'incl. 20% waste in qty').
seed_data_creation: Created 1 LP estimate via API ('TEST_LP_RawQty') — deleted in fixture teardown. The pre-existing LP estimate EST-078678 was left as found (qty restored to 194, waste restored to 20).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Line qty inputs use testid pattern: qty-{section.title}-{line.name} (e.g., 'qty-LP Smart Siding-38 Series Lap 3/8" x 8" x 16''). This testid contains quotes/spaces so use querySelector with starts-with ([data-testid^=...]) instead of full-match. To edit React inputs reliably, use the native setter + dispatch input/change events (Playwright .fill() worked but JS injection is more reliable). LP section accordions start COLLAPSED; click the header text 'LP SMART SIDING' to expand. The 'Previous read available' badge on Job Information lets the AI blueprint be restored (no fresh AI cost). All 7 LP Smart Siding rows present: 38 Lap 8" + 6", Nickel Gap, Shake, 4x8 Panel, 4x10 Panel, Vertical Panel.

verified_acceptance_criteria.T3_green_hint_visible: PASS — 'BAKED INTO LINE QTY ON IMPORT — CHANGE % TO RECOMPUTE'
verified_acceptance_criteria.T5_backend_raw_qty_roundtrip: PASS — POST kind=lp_smart waste_pct=20 → PUT line w/ raw_qty=198 qty=238 → GET preserves both fields (test_lp_waste_roundtrip.py 4/4)
verified_acceptance_criteria.T6_wasteLogic_node_tests: PASS — all 38 assertions including 9 new LP cases
verified_acceptance_criteria.T8_manual_qty_edit_preserved_on_waste_ch: PASS — set Lap qty=100 (no raw_qty), changed waste 20→25, qty stayed at 100
verified_acceptance_criteria.T9_live_hover_blueprint_reimport: SKIPPED per main-agent instruction (AI budget unconfirmed)

rca_of_the_issue: Root cause: isCutProneItem() in /app/frontend/src/lib/wasteLogic.js didn't recognize the 4 LP sections so bakeWasteIntoLines() returned LP lines unchanged on HOVER/Blueprint import — qty stored = raw blueprint value with no waste multiplier applied. Howard's 20% waste setting was silently ignored. Fix: extended the classifier with explicit branches for 'lp smart siding', 'lp smartside trim', 'lp smartside soffit', and 'lp siding accessories' (OSC-only via name match). Mitigation: existing LP estimates created BEFORE the fix have raw_qty=null and will not auto-recompute; users must click 'Read Blueprints → Restore' (or re-upload) to re-bake waste into qty. Strongly recommend the main agent add a one-time banner on legacy LP estimates prompting a re-import.
